_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national cron (Adam Johns)
_meta.note: Federal House batch — AZ-07 (Grijalva D incumbent + Butierez R challenger), AZ-08 (Hamadeh R freshman), AZ-09 (Gosar R), CA-01 (Denney D challenger). HETERODOX-D catches: Grijalva $0 AIPAC (anti-AIPAC progressive lane) + Pima Co. anti-vaccine-mandate vote (framed bodily-autonomy); Denney Track-AIPAC endorsed + anti-Big-Ag + Peace Action endorsed. HETERODOX-R catches: Hamadeh AIPAC-funded ($42K) + Iran War Powers NAY + DECLINED Freedom Caucus membership; Gosar deep restraint record ($0 AIPAC, anti-Ukraine-aid, vaccine-mandate opposition) but Iran 2026 vote ambiguous. CURRENCY: Grijalva won 9/23/2025 special, sworn 11/12/2025; Butierez LOST special 68.9-29.4 but STILL on 2026 R primary ballot; Denney lost 6/2/2026 CA-01 special (18.4%) but STILL running Nov 2026 general in redrawn D+10 seat against McGuire (D) + Gallagher (R).

set.office: U.S. Representative AZ-07 (D incumbent — won 2025 special after father Raúl Grijalva's death; sworn 11/12/2025; running for full term 2026)

profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-04 — federal evidence sweep replacing archetype_curated. Sworn 11/12/2025 (a record 50-day delay), so most 119th Congress roll calls pre-Nov 2025 are N/A. Scored from Pima County Board record (2017-2025), special-election campaign positions, post-swearing-in record + endorsements: EMILY's List + Planned Parenthood Action Fund + Reproductive Freedom for All + Giffords PAC + Everytown + Christopher Street Project (first-ever endorsement) + LPAC. HETERODOX-D catches: (1) NO AIPAC money — AIPAC publicly disengaged, she was positioned as the anti-AIPAC progressive alternative to Daniel Hernandez; (2) 2021 Pima Co. vote AGAINST employer COVID vaccine mandate citing bodily autonomy ('I have a problem with governing bodies dictating what you do with your body') — context tied to abortion-rights framing but reads as informed-consent HARD TRUE on industry_capture q0.
profile.background: U.S. Representative AZ-07 (Tucson + SE Arizona), sworn 11/12/2025. Daughter of late Rep. Raúl Grijalva (D, died 3/13/2025). 20 years on Tucson Unified School District board. Pima County Supervisor 2020-2025 (Board Chair). Won 7/15/2025 D primary, won 9/23/2025 special general 68.9-29.4 over Daniel Butierez (R). Sits on House Education & Workforce + Natural Resources committees. Signed Epstein-files discharge petition as the 218th signature the day she was sworn in.

adelita-grijalva.notes_set: AZ-07 D INCUMBENT — daughter of Raúl Grijalva, deep progressive record on TUSD board + Pima Supervisor. HARD FALSEs (cited): sanctity_of_life q0/q1 (led Pima Co. 4-1 resolution declaring abortion 'basic right' + urged providers to disregard AZ 1864 ban); q4 EMILY's List + PPAF + RFFA endorsements; biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4 Christopher Street Project pro-trans endorsement + LPAC + Washington Blade swearing-in LGBTQ reiteration; family_child_sovereignty q0 'fighting school voucher programs' + Education cmte seat to oppose school choice; q2 20-yr TUSD record opposing 'attacks on public education'; border_immigration q0 wall 'completely useless' + demands environmental review; q1 opposes 'mass deportation' + Veterans Visa Act stopping vet deportations; q2 explicitly defends sanctuary cities; self_defense q0/q1 Giffords PAC + Everytown 'unwavering gun sense champion' + Pima Co. ordinance challenging state firearms preemption + universal background checks/safe-storage/ghost-gun push. HARD TRUEs (HETERODOX-D catches, cited): foreign_policy_restraint q3 — AIPAC publicly disengaged, anti-AIPAC progressive lane positioning per Jewish Insider; industry_capture q0 — 2021 Pima Co. vote AGAINST employer vaccine mandate citing bodily autonomy. NULL across Christian liberty / economic stewardship / election integrity / industry q1-q4 (looked, no public position on Christian symbols/Sabbath/CBDC/Fed/WEF/paper ballots/Zuckerbucks/PREP-shields/Big Ag — do NOT infer from party). Pre-Nov 2025 roll calls are N/A — sworn 11/12/2025.

set.office: U.S. Representative AZ-07 (R candidate 2026 — Tucson roofer; lost 9/23/2025 special general 68.9-29.4 to Grijalva; lost 2024 general 63.4-36.6 to Raúl Grijalva)

profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-04 — federal evidence sweep replacing low_evidence. Ballotpedia confirms STILL on 2026 R primary ballot ($223,537 raised) — did not complete 2026 Candidate Connection survey. Lost 2025 special by 39.5 pts. Scored from his 2024 Ballotpedia survey + campaign-site issues page + X. Substantive content limited to six policy planks (Economy, Education, Criminal Justice, Homelessness/Drugs, 2A, Constituent Services). Abortion, marriage, gender, election integrity, foreign policy, deportation specifics — ALL ABSENT from his platform (striking omissions for a self-identified Christian R challenger). HARD TRUE on christian_liberty q0 ('Christ fights my battles'; identifies Jesus as personal role model) + general 2A-as-God-given-right signal. Many cells must remain NULL — that is the honest call.
profile.background: Tucson roofer/contractor (Butierez Roofing). Self-described ex-felon (his platform highlights his own federal prosecution for cannabis possession resulting in 10-year sentence — informs his Criminal Justice plank against DOJ overreach). Has run for AZ-07 multiple times: 2024 general (lost to Raúl Grijalva 63.4-36.6), 2025 special general (lost to Adelita Grijalva 68.9-29.4).

daniel-butierez.notes_set: AZ-07 R CHALLENGER — perennial Tucson candidate. STILL on 2026 R primary ballot. Most scoreable cells null because his platform is genuinely thin on the rubric's questions (no abortion/marriage/election/foreign-policy planks at all). HARD evidence: christian_liberty q0 (TRUE — 'Christ fights my battles and always will'; identifies Jesus as personal role model in Ballotpedia survey). Border-security rhetoric ('Fixing the damage of these open borders caused by Grijalva') is too vague to map to specific q-cells. 2A plank ('right given by God…so that every man woman and child would never be denied their right to defend themselves') is a general principle, not specific to constitutional-carry / red-flag / NFA repeal — held NULL on those cells per the sparse-data discipline. No Ballotpedia endorsements identified. Soft-left framing on Homelessness/Drugs plank (federally-funded job-training, healthcare, education) is atypical of small-government R rhetoric — flagged but not specifically scoreable.

set.office: U.S. Representative AZ-08 (R freshman — sworn 1/3/2025; previously 2022 R nominee for AZ AG, lost narrowly to Mayes; Trump-endorsed for 2026 re-election)

profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-04 — federal evidence sweep replacing archetype_party_default. 119th Congress freshman, Heritage Action 94% (119th). Committee: House Armed Services + Republican Study Committee. HETERODOX-R catches: (1) AIPAC PAC funded $42,128 + AIPAC-sponsored Netanyahu meeting Aug 2025 — HARD FALSE on foreign_policy q3; (2) Voted NO on H.Con.Res.38 Iran War Powers (6/3/2026), publicly mocked restraint coalition as 'foolish' — HARD FALSE on foreign_policy q0; (3) Explicitly DECLINED House Freedom Caucus membership, positions as 'bridge between traditional GOP and MAGA wing'. Pro-life with rape/incest/life-of-mother exceptions (not abolitionist) — q1 SOFT FALSE.
profile.background: U.S. Representative AZ-08 (West Maricopa Valley), sworn 1/3/2025. Chicago-born to Syrian (Druze) immigrant parents, raised in Arizona. Army Reserve JAG officer. Was 2022 R nominee for AZ Attorney General (lost extremely narrowly to Kris Mayes; pursued election-fraud lawsuit). House Armed Services Committee member. Republican Study Committee member. NOT a Freedom Caucus member (spokesperson confirmed declined).

set.office: U.S. Representative AZ-09 (R, in Congress since 1/3/2011 — formerly AZ-04; running 2026 re-election in R+15 district vs. Teresa Volesky in 7/21/2026 primary)

profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-04 — federal evidence sweep replacing archetype_curated. Deep tenure (since 1/3/2011) + Heritage Action 98% (119th, 118th) / 100% (117th) + NRLC 100% + NARAL 0% + NRA A + GOA 75% + NumbersUSA A+. Bioguide G000565. House Freedom Caucus member; failed America First Caucus founder. RESTRAINT-R cluster: HARD TRUEs on foreign_policy_restraint q1/q2/q3 (NAY HR 8035 Ukraine 4/20/2024, called Ukraine aid 'endless and purposeless war'; cosponsor of bill to bar Ukraine aid; $0 AIPAC PAC money per TrackAIPAC). HARD TRUEs on industry_capture q0/q1 (rare for federal R: reintroduced 'End the Vaccine Carveout Act' stripping NCVIA shields; vocally opposed COVID vaccine mandates). NOTE on q9.0: research agent flagged AMBIGUITY — Gosar was NOT among the 4 R crossover votes on H.Con.Res.38 Iran War Powers (6/3/2026); only Massie/Davidson/Barrett/Fitzpatrick crossed. He either voted NAY or was absent (clerk page returned 403). Conflicting evidence with his prior war-powers letter cosponsorship → q9.0 held NULL pending clerk confirmation.
profile.background: U.S. Representative AZ-09 (formerly AZ-04). First elected 2010, in Congress since 1/3/2011. Catholic, dentist by training (DDS). House Freedom Caucus member; founding America First Caucus attempt (2021, scrapped). Censured by the House Nov 17 2021 (223-207) over an anime video targeting Rep. Ocasio-Cortez — context, not scoreable. Family note: most of his siblings have publicly opposed him politically — context only.

profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-04 — federal evidence sweep replacing archetype_curated. CRITICAL currency fix: LaMalfa died 1/6/2026 (aortic dissection); James Gallagher (R) won the 6/2/2026 special general on OLD map 61.6%, Denney got 18.4%. District was redrawn D+10 for Nov 2026 — Denney still running, opponents Mike McGuire (D) + Gallagher (R). Substantial policy content (former ag instructor + 3-cycle D nominee) — NOT roster-verified-only. Scored from campaign-site issues page + endorsements + local press. HETERODOX-D catches: (1) Track AIPAC endorsement (HARD TRUE on foreign_policy q3 — extremely rare D crossover); (2) Anti-Big-Ag family-farm-first policy (HARD TRUE on industry_capture q2 — genuine alignment from ag-instructor background); (3) Peace Action + Organize for Peace endorsements + 'national security not built by bombs' diplomacy frame + denounced Venezuela incursion (SOFT restraint on q0/q1); (4) Said Hakeem Jeffries needs replacement (intra-D heterodoxy signal, not specifically scoreable). HARD FALSEs: EMILY's List endorsement (q1.4); explicit pro-trans 'I stand proudly and unapologetically with the LGBTQ+ community including transgender' (q2.2/q2.4); explicit GVPO red-flag-law platform (q8.1).
profile.background: Former agriculture instructor + nonprofit ag consultant in Chico, CA. 3-cycle CA-01 D nominee: lost 2018 to LaMalfa 56.1-43.9; lost 2020 ~58-42; lost 6/2/2026 special 61.6 (Gallagher-R) — Denney 18.4 / 20,500 votes. Now running Nov 2026 general on redrawn CA-01 (D+10) against Mike McGuire (D) + James Gallagher (R).

audrey-denney.notes_set: CA-01 D CHALLENGER (3-cycle nominee) — substantial policy content. HARD FALSEs (cited): sanctity_of_life q4 (EMILY's List endorsed 11/2025); biblical_marriage q2 'I stand proudly and unapologetically with the LGBTQ+ community, including transgender and gender non-conforming people' + q4 ('From LGBTQ+ rights to racial justice'); border_immigration q0 (denounced Trump enforcement, called for 'drastic overhaul of ICE') + q1 (opposes enforcement causing 'fear and disruption'); self_defense q1 EXPLICIT — campaign Issues page: 'Expand Gun Violence Protection Orders (GVPOs), allowing families to petition courts to temporarily remove firearms' = literal red-flag-law platform. HETERODOX-D HARD TRUEs (cited): foreign_policy_restraint q2 ('yes' when asked if Israel is committing genocide in Gaza; questions Israel aid); q3 (Track AIPAC endorsement — anti-AIPAC); industry_capture q2 ('I want to help change from favoring large corporate farmers to putting family farmers in rural communities at the center'). SOFT TRUEs: foreign_policy q0 ('True national security is not built by bombs. It is built through diplomacy') + q1 (Peace Action + Organize for Peace endorsements, denounced Venezuela incursion); industry_capture q3 (regenerative-ag advocate, family-farm-centered policy — though no specific raw-milk/USDA-overreach quote); economic_stewardship q4 (anti-corporate-PAC, 'corporate greed is entrenched' — alignment is from progressive left, not Christian-conservative, but lines up with anti-financial-cartel position). NULL across family/child sovereignty (no school choice, parental notification, CRT/SOGI/porn AV, faith-based adoption record), christian liberty, economic q0-q3, election integrity, border q3/q4, self_defense q3/q4, foreign q4, industry q0/q1/q4. Notable endorsement absences: NO Planned Parenthood, Sierra Club, J Street, or Everytown — unusual gaps for a progressive D.
